moment_text: I eventually evolved into social work because my other passion in college honestly ended up being sociology. And I always like to say that social work is as if psychology and sociology had a baby. Psychology is very much oriented on like. The introspective, the what's going on in your brain right now? Not always, but as a general rule and sociology is about how do systems affect a person. Like, doesn't really matter as much what's going on in your head. It really matters. Like how does culture, how does the macro affect you? And I was like, I think both actually affect you very much. And that's kind of what social work is. It's looking at both the inner workings as, as well as the socials, social goings on of the time.
eligibility_rationale: The moment contains agency signals (making_decisions, seeking_information) and a system interaction (education_system), meeting the evidence gate requirements.
notes_for_reviewer: This moment clearly defines the speaker's professional philosophy, which is foundational to her approach.

moment_text: I think I was honestly very intrigued, especially by the criticisms of psychology. Like I took a cross-cultural psychology class with a wonderful professor who had like a big hand in steering my success in college, and it was all about how a lot of it's. Experiments and a lot of like, the, the things we take for granted when it comes to psychology are very focused on the one population in particular, which, you know, honestly is white, straight, cis male, uh, college educated actually as well. A lot of the experiments that we take for granted, like the Stanford Prison Experiment are all based on that. Population. And of course we live in a very diverse world, even just in our country, but especially across the world. And a lot of populations are going unstudied and therefore under cared for. So the cross-cultural piece of it, the cultural humility, the cultural competence, really I latched onto. And uh, that kind of opened my brain to especially, you know, LGBT, neurodivergent, et cetera. So that was really something I oriented on.
